title: Disorders of Calcium and Phosphorus Metabolism
authors: Kelsey B. Eitel, Ildiko H. Koves, Kathryn D. Ness & Parisa Salehi

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Disorders of Calcium and Phosphorus Metabolism?
Ca/P disorders พบบ่อยมากใน NICU — จาก asymptomatic hypocalcemia ใน preterm ไปถึง life-threatening hypercalcemia ใน NSHPT. สิ่งสำคัญ 6 ข้อ: (1) Early hypocalcemia (<48 hr): prematurity, asphyxia, IDM, IUGR — แก้ด้วย Ca gluconate IV; (2) Late hypocalcemia (>3–5 d): hypoparathyroidism, high-phosphate load, Mg deficiency — DiGeorge 22q11.2 deletion ต้องนึกถึง; (3) Subcutaneous fat necrosis (SFN): complication ของ therapeutic hypothermia — screen Ca ที่ 30, 45, 60 d หลัง lesion; (4) NSHPT (CaSR homozygous mutation): critically ill neonate → total parathyroidectomy; (5) MBD of prematurity: เกิดจาก phosphate + calcium deficiency — ALP + P ที่ 4–6 wk ใน VLBW; (6) Ionized Ca²⁺ คือ biologically active fraction — วัด iCa ใน sick infants เสมอ, อย่าพึ่งแต่ total Ca.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย PTH–1,25(OH)₂D axis ใน Ca/P homeostasis: กลไก, feedback loop, และ role ของ CaSR
ระบุ definition ของ neonatal hypocalcemia แยกตาม GA: term/preterm >1500 g vs VLBW
แยก early (<48 hr) vs late (>3–5 d) neonatal hypocalcemia: etiology, clinical setting, workup
วินิจฉัย DiGeorge syndrome (22q11.2 deletion): features, genetics, Ca monitoring
รู้ hypoparathyroid syndromes ที่สำคัญ: Kenny-Caffey, Sanjad-Sakati, ADH1/ADH2, pseudohypoparathyroidism
อธิบาย hypocalcemia จาก hypomagnesemia: กลไก, HOMG1 (TRPM6), รักษาด้วย Mg ก่อน
รู้ causes ของ neonatal hypercalcemia: NSHPT, FHH, Williams syndrome, IIH, SFN, iatrogenic
อธิบาย SFN: risk factors, hypercalcemia timing, monitoring schedule, treatment
อธิบาย MBD of prematurity: pathophysiology, lab markers (ALP, P, PTH), รักษาด้วย fortified nutrition
รู้ inherited MBD: VDDR-1A (CYP27B1), VDDR-1B (CYPR1), VDR defect, XLH (PHEX), OI, hypophosphatasia
คำนวณ corrected Ca และรู้ management ของ hypocalcemic crisis: Ca gluconate 100–200 mg/kg IV
1. Physiology — Ca, P, and Mg Homeostasis
1.1 Calcium Compartments & Normal Values
Total plasma Ca: 55–60% diffusible (ionized + complexed); 40–45% protein-bound. Ca²⁺ = biologically active fraction — regulated via CaSR
Normal total serum Ca (after day 2–3): 8.8–10.6 mg/dL (2.2–2.6 mmol/L); ionized Ca²⁺: 4–5.6 mg/dL (1–1.4 mmol/L)
Hypoalbuminemia → ↓ total Ca แต่ iCa²⁺ มักปกติ (เพราะ protein-bound fraction ลดลง). Correction formula: corrected Ca = [0.8 × (normal albumin − patient albumin)] + serum Ca
Alkalosis (bicarbonate infusion, hyperventilation) → ↑ albumin binding → ↓ iCa²⁺ โดยไม่เปลี่ยน total Ca. Exchange transfusion, ECMO → citrate chelation → acute ↓ iCa²⁺
1.2 PTH–Vitamin D Axis

PTH (9500-Da single-chain polypeptide): สังเคราะห์โดย 4 parathyroid glands (embryonic 3rd + 4th pharyngeal pouches). Half-life < 4 min. Function: ↑ Ca reabsorption kidney + ↑ bone resorption + ↑ 1,25(OH)₂D synthesis + ↓ P reabsorption (↓ TmP/GFR)

CaSR (Ca²⁺-sensing receptor): detects changes as small as 0.025–0.05 mmol/L; setpoint = Ca²⁺ 1.2–1.25 mmol/L ที่ PTH secretion ½ maximal. Loss-of-function CaSR → NSHPT/FHH; gain-of-function → ADH1 (autosomal dominant hypocalcemia)

Vitamin D pathway: skin (7-dehydrocholesterol) → previtamin D → vitamin D → liver (25-hydroxylase) → 25(OH)D (store marker) → kidney (1α-hydroxylase) → 1,25(OH)₂D (active, calcitriol). Normal 25(OH)D: 10–50 ng/mL; 1,25(OH)₂D: 30–75 pg/mL
1,25(OH)₂D: ↑ intestinal Ca + P absorption; ↑ bone resorption (via osteoclasts); ↑ renal Ca reabsorption. Suppresses PTH gene expression (long-loop negative feedback)

PTHrP (PTH-related protein): homologous N-terminal to PTH; binds same PTHR1 receptor; important in fetal/placental Ca transport and lactating breast; secreted into breast milk at 10,000× serum concentration
Calcitonin: thyroid parafollicular C-cells; ↓ serum Ca (antiresorptive + ↑ renal Ca excretion); นวนใน newborns สูงมาก. May have calcitropic function in neonate
1.3 Phosphorus Homeostasis
Normal serum P: infants 4.5–9.3 mg/dL; adults 3.0–4.5 mg/dL. Higher in infants = ↑ tubular P resorption (↑ TmP/GFR)
TmP/GFR = 'setpoint' ที่กำหนด fasting serum P; regulated by PTH, PTHrP, FGF23 (→ ↓ TmP/GFR), growth hormone
FGF23: bone-derived hormone → ↓ renal P reabsorption (↓ TmP/GFR) + ↓ 1,25(OH)₂D. Elevated in XLH, autosomal dominant hypophosphatemic rickets
1.4 Perinatal Mineral Metabolism
Third trimester: placenta transfers ~30 g Ca + >16 g P; fetal Ca accretion ~140–150 mg/kg/day. PTHrP (placental mid-molecule) drives transplacental Ca pump via TRPV6 channel
At birth: placental Ca transfer ceases abruptly → total Ca falls from ~11 mg/dL to 8–9 mg/dL by 24–48 hr; iCa²⁺ nadir 4.4–5.4 mg/dL. Concomitant ↑ PTH + 1,25(OH)₂D stabilizes Ca
Fetal PTH: relatively low; calcitonin + PTHrP: relatively high. By 2 weeks, serum Ca normalizes to adult range
2. Neonatal Hypocalcemia — Definition, Presentation, Classification
2.1 Definition
Term infants and preterm >1500 g: total Ca < 8 mg/dL (2 mmol/L) หรือ iCa²⁺ < 4.4 mg/dL (1.10 mmol/L)
VLBW infants: total Ca < 7 mg/dL (1.75 mmol/L) หรือ iCa²⁺ < 4 mg/dL (1 mmol/L)
ใน sick infants: วัด iCa²⁺ โดยตรงจาก freshly obtained blood — มีความแม่นยำกว่า total Ca ใน setting ที่มี acid-base หรือ albumin abnormalities
2.2 Clinical Presentation
Most infants: asymptomatic. Signs ไม่สัมพันธ์กับ magnitude ของ Ca fall เสมอไป
Neuromuscular excitability (tetany): jitteriness, muscle jerks/twitches, hyperactivity; poor feeding, hypotonia, apnea, tachycardia/tachypnea, high-pitched cry, irritability
Seizures: focal หรือ generalized clonic; stridor (laryngospasm), wheezing (bronchospasm), vomiting (pylorospasm — may mimic intestinal obstruction)
ECG: prolonged QT interval — characteristic; Chvostek sign ไม่ reliable ใน neonates
2.3 Early Neonatal Hypocalcemia (<48 hr)
Timing: peak ที่ 24–48 hr. Exaggeration ของ normal postnatal Ca decline
Classic 4 settings: (1) Prematurity — (2) Perinatal asphyxia/stress — (3) IDM — (4) IUGR
Prematurity: magnitude inversely proportional to GA; ~1/3 preterm + most VLBW มี total Ca < 7 mg/dL ใน 2 วันแรก. iCa2+ 'spared' เพราะ ↓ protein + ↓ pH; มักไม่มี symptoms
Asphyxia/stress: hyperphosphatemia (tissue catabolism + renal insufficiency + acidosis) + exaggerated calcitonin response + ↓ PTH. Low iCa²⁺ + ↑ Mg = marker ของ severe HIE + poor outcome
IDM: ≥20–50% ของ IDM เป็น hypocalcemia; onset 24–72 hr; Mg deficiency (maternal glycosuria → urinary Mg loss) contributes → ↓ PTH production + action
IUGR: ↓ transplacental Ca transport due to uteroplacental insufficiency
Additional causes: phototherapy, maternal anticonvulsants (↑ 25(OH)D catabolism), citrated blood transfusion, furosemide, maternal hyperparathyroidism
2.4 Late Neonatal Hypocalcemia (>3–5 days)
ขึ้นปลายสัปดาห์แรก; พบบ่อยกว่าใน term infants; ไม่สัมพันธ์กับ DM/asphyxia

Classic cause: high phosphate load (cow's milk formula) → ↑ P → ↑ Ca deposition + ↓ PTH → hypocalcemia + hyperphosphatemia. Mechanism: ↓ TmP/GFR maturity + transient ↓ PTH + hypomagnesemia + ↓ maternal vitamin D
Late hypocalcemia + hyperphosphatemia → thorough investigation always warranted: hypoparathyroid syndrome, hypomagnesemia, renal insufficiency, vitamin D disorder
3. Neonatal Hypoparathyroid Syndromes
3.1 DiGeorge Syndrome (DGS) — 22q11.2 Deletion
Incidence: 1:4,000 live births; sporadic หรือ autosomal dominant (variable penetrance). Most common cause ของ congenital hypoparathyroidism
Mechanism: failure of migration of neural crest cells into 3rd and 4th pharyngeal pouches → TBX1 haploinsufficiency (transcription factor on 22q11.2)
Classic triad: (1) Hypoparathyroid hypocalcemia (2) Thymic hypoplasia → T-cell deficiency (3) Conotruncal cardiac defects (pulmonary atresia/TOF, type B interrupted aortic arch, truncus arteriosus)
Craniofacial: microretrognathia, mandibular hypoplasia, submucous cleft palate, low-set abnormal pinnae, telecanthus, short philtrum, small mouth
Diagnosis: array-CGH (most sensitive) > FISH (22q11 probes) > G-band karyotype. นึกถึงเสมอเมื่อ cardiac outflow tract anomaly แม้ไม่มี DGS features อื่น
IMPORTANT: normal PTH ขณะ normocalcemic ≠ exclude DGS; absent thymic shadow ≠ reliable indicator. DGS อาจ resolve hypoparathyroidism ใน childhood แต่ PTH reserve อาจไม่เพียงพอเมื่อ stressed
3.2 Other Hypoparathyroid Syndromes

rows: IUGR, transient hypoparathyroidism, short stature, macrocephaly, cortical thickening tubular bones, ocular abnormalities; NO developmental delay in AD form
Congenital hypoparathyroidism + severe growth failure + dysmorphic facies (deep-set eyes, depressed nasal bridge, large floppy earlobes) + small hands/feet + developmental delay
Hypoparathyroidism + sensorineural Deafness + Renal dysplasia; wide phenotypic variability; 60% renal disease, only 9% ESRD
GATA3 = transcription factor for parathyroid, thymus, kidney, inner ear, CNS
Hypoparathyroidism + adrenal insufficiency + mucocutaneous candidiasis; AR; candidiasis appears first (<5 yr), hypoparathyroidism by <10 yr
Parathyroid hemorrhage ('parathyroid apoplexy') = common → contributes to early death

blocks.text: 3.3 Autosomal Dominant Hypocalcemia (ADH) — Gain-of-Function CaSR/GNA11

ADH1: gain-of-function CaSR mutations (chr 3q13.3–q21) → ↑ sensitivity to extracellular Ca²⁺ → ↓ PTH secretion + ↓ renal Ca reabsorption → hypocalcemia + relative hypercalciuria / nephrocalcinosis risk
ADH2: gain-of-function GNA11 mutations (chr 19p13) → ↑ Gα11 sensitivity → ↓ PTH; distinguished by short stature + NO hypercalciuria compared to ADH1
Most ADH asymptomatic; Ca usually 6–8 mg/dL; Treatment goal: Ca low-normal (8.0–8.5 mg/dL) to prevent nephrocalcinosis. Monitor urinary Ca/Cr ratio within 2 wk of starting calcitriol

⚠️ ADH1 = Hypocalcemia ที่ CONTRAINDICATE การ push Ca จนปกติ
ADH1 (CaSR gain-of-function): ถ้าแก้ Ca ให้ปกติ → hypercalciuria + nephrocalcinosis + renal impairment. Goal: Ca low-normal range (8.0–8.5 mg/dL). ต้อง distinguish ADH1 จาก isolated hypoparathyroidism ด้วย CaSR gene mutation analysis — key feature: hypercalciuria แม้ Ca ยังต่ำกว่าปกติ
3.4 Pseudohypoparathyroidism

PHP type 1A (Albright hereditary osteodystrophy — AHO): loss of one GNAS1 allele (Gsα) → PTH resistance. Features: hypocalcemia + hyperphosphatemia + ↑↑ PTH + short stature, obesity, round face, brachymetacarpalism, subcutaneous calcifications + multihormone resistance (TSH, GnRH, GH)
PHP type 1B: imprinting defect ใน GNAS1 (both alleles unmethylated/paternal pattern) → isolated PTH resistance, NO AHO somatic phenotype
Transient neonatal pseudohypoparathyroidism: occasionally reported; most PHP1 patients ไม่ hypocalcemic ใน first month of life
4. Hypomagnesemia and Hypocalcemia
Mechanism: Mg required for CaSR-mediated PTH secretion + end-organ PTH response. Hypomagnesemia → ↓ PTH secretion + PTH resistance → hypocalcemia ที่ไม่ respond to Ca alone

HOMG1 (Primary hypomagnesemia with secondary hypocalcemia): mutations in TRPM6 gene (transient receptor potential channel; intestinal + renal tubular expression) → defective intestinal Mg absorption + renal Mg leak. AR. Persistent hypocalcemia + tetany + seizures uncontrolled by anticonvulsants/Ca → นึกถึง HOMG1
Treatment HOMG1: IV Mg sulfate ก่อน (50 mg/kg), ตาม oral Mg high-dose lifelong. Low serum Mg normalizes PTH + Ca
Renal hypomagnesemia with hypercalciuria + nephrocalcinosis: mutations in paracellin-1 (CLDN16) gene on chr 3q27; AR; 50% require renal replacement in 2nd decade
Transient neonatal hypomagnesemia: occurs with hypocalcemia; Mg typically 0.8–1.4 mg/dL (less severe); treat with Mg if Ca doesn't normalize with Ca supplements alone
Secondary hypomagnesemia: loop diuretics, aminoglycosides, amphotericin B → renal Mg wasting; hypokalemia ไม่แก้โดยไม่ให้ Mg ก่อน

PEARL: Hypocalcemia ที่ไม่ respond to calcium → check Magnesium
ถ้าให้ Ca gluconate IV แล้ว hypocalcemia persist/recur → ส่ง serum Mg ทันที. Hypomagnesemia inhibits PTH secretion and action → Ca ไม่ขึ้นไม่ว่าจะให้ Ca เท่าไหร่. รักษา Mg ก่อน → Ca จะขึ้นตาม. HOMG1 (TRPM6 mutations) = permanent Mg wasting → lifelong oral Mg supplement. อย่าลืม: hypokalemia ร่วมกับ Mg depletion ไม่แก้ได้ด้วย K+ เพียงอย่างเดียว.
5. Management of Neonatal Hypocalcemia
5.1 When to Treat
Asymptomatic preterm + not ill from other cause → probably no specific treatment needed; early hypocalcemia should resolve by day 3

Treat when: (1) symptomatic (seizures, tetany, apnea); (2) iCa²⁺ < 2.5–3.0 mg/dL; (3) total Ca < 6.0–6.5 mg/dL ใน preterm. CV compromise (respiratory distress, PH, asphyxia, sepsis) → monitor iCa²⁺ closely
5.2 IV Calcium Gluconate — Doses

rows: 100–200 mg/kg Ca gluconate IV over 5–10 min; repeat in 10 min if no response
Monitor ECG QT; stop if bradycardia; max rate caution
Causes hyperchloremic acidosis in high doses
45–75 mg/kg/day elemental Ca gluconate continuous infusion
Target Ca 7–8 mg/dL; add to IV fluids
50–150 mg/kg/day elemental Ca gluconate in 4–6 divided doses; OR Ca glubionate 30–50 mg/kg
Ca gluconate better tolerated (Ca glubionate: high osmolality → GI irritation)
Ca:P ratio 4:1 in feeds; low-P formula (human milk / Similac PM 60/40) + Ca supplement

blocks.text:
⚠️ IV Calcium Safety Rules
(1) Bolus IV Ca → bradyarrhythmia — monitor ECG; bolus only for emergency. (2) Extravasation → skin/subcutaneous necrosis + calcification — meticulous IV care. (3) Umbilical vein catheter ≠ at IVC → hepatic necrosis; umbilical artery → aortic spasm + intestinal necrosis. (4) Do NOT mix Ca with phosphate or bicarbonate — precipitates.
5.3 Magnesium Administration
IM/IV: 50% MgSO₄ = 4 mEq/mL. Dose: 25–50 mg/kg (50% MgSO₄); repeat q 12–24 hr. IV: administer slowly with ECG monitoring (prolonged AV conduction, AV block)
Transient hypomagnesemia: often 1–2 injections sufficient. HOMG1: permanent → lifelong high-dose oral Mg
5.4 Vitamin D Treatment
Late hypocalcemia with VD-deficiency rickets: vitamin D 1000–2000 IU/day oral × 4 weeks + ≥40 mg/kg/day elemental Ca (to prevent 'hungry bones' syndrome)

Hypoparathyroidism / renal failure / VDDR: calcitriol (1,25[OH]₂D) 0.25–1 μg/day; adjust q 2–4 weeks. Target Ca low-normal (8.0–8.5 mg/dL). Monitor UCa/Cr within 2 wk; periodic renal US for nephrocalcinosis
6. Neonatal Hypercalcemia
6.1 Definition and Symptoms
Definition: total Ca > 11.0 mg/dL + iCa²⁺ > 5.0 mg/dL

Mild (Ca 11–12): may be asymptomatic; moderate (Ca 12–14): poor weight gain, poor feeding, constipation, polyuria (nephrogenic DI from impaired concentrating ability); severe (Ca >14): vomiting, respiratory distress, apnea, hypotonia, lethargy, seizures
ECG: shortened QT interval (opposite of hypocalcemia). HTN from vasoconstriction effects. Chronic: nephrocalcinosis, nephrolithiasis, osteitis fibrosa cystica
6.2 CaSR-Mutation Syndromes

rows: Pamidronate 0.5–1 mg/kg + cinacalcet 0.4–2 mg/kg/day → total parathyroidectomy
CaSR heterozygous (AD); loss-of-function; chr 3q21.1; >200 mutations known
Mild ↑ Ca, ↓ UCa/Cr (<0.01), normal/mild ↑ PTH, ↑ Mg; asymptomatic usually
Usually no treatment; genetic testing for counseling (parents carry → child risk NSHPT)
Elevated PTH, ↓ P, osteomalacia; more severe Ca elevation
Monitor; genetic testing all suspected FHH

blocks.text: 6.3 Williams Syndrome & Idiopathic Infantile Hypercalcemia (IIH)

Williams syndrome (7q11.23 deletion including ELN gene): 15–45% have idiopathic hypercalcemia; resolves by 2–4 yr. Mechanism: ↑ gut Ca absorption. Features: broad forehead, stellate iris, short nose, malar flattening, supravalvular aortic stenosis, peripheral PS, hypotonia, intellectual disability, social personality. Screen Ca + UCa/Cr early in infancy. Treatment: ↓ Ca + vit D intake + ↑ fluid; severe → calcitonin, glucocorticoids, pamidronate

IIH type 1: CYP24A1 mutations (AR) — ↓ 25-hydroxyvitamin D 24-hydroxylase → ↓ degradation of 1,25(OH)₂D → ↑↑ 1,25(OH)₂D. Profile: ↑ Ca, ↑ UCa, ↓ PTH, ↑ 1,25(OH)₂D, ↑ 25-OHD/24,25(OH)₂D ratio. Avoid vitamin D supplementation; if persistent → bisphosphonates / ketoconazole / rifampin

IIH type 2: SLC34A1 mutations (NaPi-IIa cotransporter) — urinary P wasting → ↓ FGF23 → ↑ 1,25(OH)₂D + ↑ intestinal Ca absorption; hyperphosphaturia + kidney stones. Treatment: phosphate supplementation
6.4 Subcutaneous Fat Necrosis (SFN)
Panniculitis ใน term/postterm infants; lesions: erythematous-violaceous indurated nodules/plaques — back, head, arms; appear mean day 4; resolve ใน first few months

Risk factors: birth trauma, asphyxia, meconium aspiration, infections; therapeutic hypothermia (cooling blanket areas); maternal: gestational DM, preeclampsia, cocaine/Ca blocker use, high thrombosis risk

Hypercalcemia mechanism: uncontrolled 1,25(OH)₂D production by activated macrophages in necrotic fat + ↑ PTH + prostaglandin E → ↑ bone resorption. Hypercalcemia may appear 1–2 months after delivery; majority < 28 days post-lesions; some >70 days
Monitoring protocol: screen Ca at diagnosis of lesions + at 30, 45, 60 days after resolution. Nephrocalcinosis may persist but ไม่ดูเหมือนจะส่งผลเสียต่อ renal outcomes ระยะยาว
Treatment: hydration + furosemide + low-Ca/low-VD diet. Highly responsive to glucocorticoids; severe/refractory → bisphosphonates
6.5 Treatment of Hypercalcemia

Mild–moderate: ↓ Ca + VD intake; ↑ fluid. Moderate–severe: IV normal saline → induce sodium diuresis → ↑ urine Ca; furosemide 1 mg/kg IV q 6–8 hr (after adequate hydration); hydrocortisone 1 mg/kg q 6 hr (↓ intestinal Ca absorption)
Calcitonin: 3–6 μg/kg SC/IM q 6 hr — ↓ bone resorption + ↑ renal Ca excretion; effective only few days (tachyphylaxis)
Bisphosphonates: pamidronate 0.5–2 mg/kg IV; zoledronic acid (3rd generation): faster + longer duration; caution in renal disease; neonatal dose 0.0125–0.025 mg/kg q 3 months
Cinacalcet: calcimimetic → ↑ CaSR sensitivity; used in CaSR mutations (NSHPT, FHH) preoperatively
7. Metabolic Bone Disease (MBD) of Prematurity
7.1 Pathophysiology
Primary cause: phosphate + calcium deficiency. 80% ของ fetal bone mineralization เกิดใน 3rd trimester (Ca 100–130 + P 60–75 mg/kg/day). Preterm infants miss this critical period

Additional risk factors: early withdrawal of placental estradiol/progesterone, immobility, NEC, medications: dexamethasone (↓ osteoblasts, ↑ osteoclasts), loop diuretics (↑ urinary Ca), methylxanthines (↑ urinary Ca), anticonvulsants (↑ 25(OH)D catabolism), PPIs/H2 blockers, anticoagulants, cyclosporine

Phosphate depletion (unsupplemented human milk): hypophosphatemia + hypophosphaturia + hypercalcemia + hypercalciuria. ↑ 1,25(OH)₂D (from ↓ P) → ↑ Ca absorption → Ca cannot be deposited (no P) → ↑ serum Ca → ↓ PTH
MBD does NOT respond to vitamin D alone unless VD deficiency is also present. VD without correcting P deficiency → aggravates hypercalcemia + hypercalciuria
7.2 Clinical Presentation and Diagnosis
Osteopenia ± rickets at growth plate: appears 6–16 weeks of age in VLBW. Fractures (ribs + long bones) in ~10% of preterm infants; pain on handling often only sign
Clinical signs: widened anterior fontanel, craniotabes, bony expansion of wrists, costochondral beading, rib fractures → respiratory distress/atelectasis

Radiology: 'osteopenia' ไม่ชัดใน plain film จนถึง 20–40% mineralization loss. Zone of provisional calcification: lucency + widening metaphysis-epiphysis; irregularity/cupping/fraying of metaphyseal margin

ALP monitoring: AAP recommendation — measure ALP + serum P at 4–6 weeks after birth in VLBW, biweekly thereafter. ALP peaks 400–800 IU/L ใน MBD. PTH > 180 mg/dL at 3 weeks = alert for MBD (Moreira et al. J Perinatol 2015)
DXA: accurate, reproducible, low radiation — limited by ไม่มี normative data ใน preterm ที่ดีพอ
7.3 Treatment and Nutrition

Enteral goals for VLBW: Ca 150–220 mg/kg/day + P 75–140 mg/kg/day — needs fortified breastmilk หรือ preterm formula (standard formula ไม่เพียงพอ). 10% to 20% ของ BW <1000 g ยังมี radiographic rickets แม้ fortify
Phosphate repletion → promotes bone mineralization → serum Ca may fall → 'hungry bones' syndrome: supplement Ca 20–60 mg/kg/day ร่วมด้วย

Serum 25(OH)D: < 6 ng/mL = severe VD deficiency; ≥20 ng/mL = sufficient. VLBW: AAP: 200–400 IU VD/day (increase to 400 IU when >1500–2000 g on full feeds); European guidelines: 800–1000 IU/day for VLBW
8. Inherited Metabolic Bone Disease

rows: CYPR1 (25-hydroxylase) — AR; rarer, milder; presents 2–7 yr
VDR gene mutations — end-organ resistance to 1,25(OH)₂D; early-onset rickets + alopecia
Supraphysiologic 1,25(OH)₂D + Ca; variable response by genotype
↓ P, normal/mild ↓ Ca, ↑ ALP, inappropriately normal 1,25(OH)₂D
Phosphate + calcitriol; burosumab (anti-FGF23 mAb) approved ≥6 months
COL1A1/COL1A2 + others (AD/AR); multiple OI types; fractures at/after birth
ALPL mutations (TNSALP) — very low ALP; AR; perinatal lethal form: respiratory failure + severe undermineralization + ↑ Ca + ↑ P
↓↓↓ ALP; Ca/P elevated in severe forms
Asfotase alfa (enzyme replacement) — improves survival in perinatal/infantile forms
Osteoclast formation defects (AR); ↑ bone density; jitteriness/seizures from hypocalcemia
↑ bone density on XR; hypocalcemia; frontal bossing; rachitic changes
Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation — usually fatal <10 yr without

blocks.text: 9. NICU Practical — Ca/P Problems ใน NICU
Jittery term infant, 36 hr: ส่ง iCa²⁺ + glucose ก่อน; ถ้า iCa²⁺ < 4.4 mg/dL → Ca gluconate 10% 100–200 mg/kg IV slow + ECG monitoring; ตรวจ maternal serum Ca (maternal HPT?); check Mg

Late hypocalcemia week 1 + hyperphosphatemia: workup → PTH (inappropriately low? → hypoparathyroidism), 25(OH)D + 1,25(OH)₂D, Mg, cardiac echo (DGS?), chromosomal microarray (22q11.2). DiGeorge: nึกถึงทุกครั้งที่มี congenital heart defect
Cooling hypothermia baby + SFN lesions: screen Ca ที่ time of diagnosis + วันที่ 30, 45, 60 หลัง lesion resolve. Low-Ca/low-VD diet + hydration + furosemide; สงวนสิทธิ์ glucocorticoids ถ้า severe

VLBW infant week 6: ส่ง ALP + serum P (AAP recommendation). ถ้า ALP > 800 + P ต่ำ → MBD → ปรับ nutrition (fortify) + เพิ่ม P supplement + Ca; อย่าเพิ่ม VD โดยไม่แก้ P ก่อน (จะทำให้ hypercalcemia แย่ลง)
Persistent hypocalcemia ไม่ respond to Ca: ส่ง serum Mg; ถ้าต่ำ → 50% MgSO₄ 25–50 mg/kg IV slow + ECG; ตาม oral Mg. HOMG1 = lifelong requirement

Preterm + suspected hypoparathyroidism: ส่ง intact PTH + Ca + P (หลัง EDTA-free tube); ถ้า PTH inappropriately low/normal + hypocalcemia + hyperphosphatemia → genetic workup; calcitriol 0.25–1 μg/day; monitor UCa/Cr ป้องกัน nephrocalcinosis

Pharmacology reminder: furosemide prolonged → bone demineralization + renal dysfunction. Glucocorticoids prolonged → osteoporosis (↓ bone formation + ↑ bone resorption). ดูแล mineral balance ใน infants on these drugs
10. 5 Bedside Pearls — สำหรับพรุ่งนี้

PEARL 1 — Late Hypocalcemia + Congenital Heart Defect = DiGeorge จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่
Term infant สัปดาห์แรก มี hypocalcemia + hyperphosphatemia + conotruncal cardiac defect → DiGeorge (22q11.2 deletion) จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่. ส่ง array-CGH ทันที. PTH อาจ normal ขณะ normocalcemic — ไม่ exclude DGS. Thymic shadow ใน CXR ไม่ใช่ reliable indicator. Screen parents สำหรับ carrier status.

PEARL 2 — Hypocalcemia ที่ไม่ respond to Calcium → Magnesium ก่อนเสมอ
ถ้าให้ Ca gluconate IV แล้ว Ca ไม่ขึ้น → check serum Mg ทันที. Mg deficiency → ↓ PTH secretion + PTH resistance → Ca จะไม่ขึ้นไม่ว่าจะให้ Ca เท่าไหร่. รักษา: 50% MgSO₄ 25–50 mg/kg IV slow. HOMG1 (TRPM6): permanent Mg wasting → lifelong oral Mg. Hypokalemia ไม่แก้โดยไม่ให้ Mg ก่อน.

PEARL 3 — SFN + Therapeutic Hypothermia = Screen Calcium ถึง 60 วัน
Therapeutic hypothermia = risk factor สำคัญสำหรับ SFN. SFN → macrophage activation → ↑ 1,25(OH)₂D → hypercalcemia ที่อาจเกิด 1–2 เดือนหลัง delivery. Screen protocol: screen Ca ที่ diagnosis + วันที่ 30, 45, 60 หลัง lesion resolve. Treatment: hydration + furosemide + low-Ca/low-VD diet → glucocorticoids → bisphosphonates if refractory.

PEARL 4 — MBD of Prematurity: Treat the Phosphate, Not the Vitamin D
VLBW ใน unsupplemented human milk → P deficiency → ↑ 1,25(OH)₂D → ↑ Ca absorption → hypercalcemia + hypercalciuria + ↓ PTH. อย่าให้ vitamin D โดยไม่แก้ P — จะทำให้ hypercalcemia แย่ลง. Treat: fortified breastmilk/preterm formula + phosphate supplement. ALP > 800 IU/L + ↓ P at week 6 = MBD. Hungry bones: เพิ่ม Ca 20–60 mg/kg/day พร้อมกับ phosphate repletion.

PEARL 5 — ADH1 (CaSR Gain-of-Function) = ห้าม Push Calcium ให้ปกติ
Autosomal dominant hypocalcemia type 1: gain-of-function CaSR → parathyroid + renal calcistat set downward → hypocalcemia + hypercalciuria. ถ้า treat ให้ Ca ปกติ → nephrocalcinosis + renal impairment. Goal: Ca low-normal 8.0–8.5 mg/dL ด้วย calcitriol + monitor UCa/Cr q 2 wk. แยกจาก isolated hypoparathyroidism ด้วย CaSR mutation analysis.
11. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Thomas TC et al. Pediatrics 2012;129:e1461 (NEJM group): Transient neonatal hypocalcemia — 70 infants; most asymptomatic; early hypocalcemia resolved by day 3; neurologic outcomes generally favorable for nutritional cause.

Abrams SA. Pediatrics 2013;131:e1676 (AAP Committee on Nutrition): Calcium and vitamin D requirements for enterally fed preterm infants — enteral Ca 150–220 + P 75–140 mg/kg/day; VD 200–400 IU/day VLBW; screen ALP + P at 4–6 wk.

Munns CF et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2016;101:394 (Global Consensus): Global consensus on prevention and management of nutritional rickets — vitamin D deficiency commonest cause globally; calcium deficiency major cause in developing countries; VD 400 IU/day prevents congenital rickets.

Stefanko NS, Drolet BA. Pediatr Dermatol 2019;36:24 (Systematic Review): Subcutaneous fat necrosis and hypercalcemia — majority of hypercalcemia cases within 28 days of lesion appearance; screen at 30, 45, 60 days; nephrocalcinosis does not seem to cause adverse renal outcomes long-term.

Schlingmann KP et al. NEJM 2011;365:410: CYP24A1 mutations and idiopathic infantile hypercalcemia — identified CYP24A1 as cause of IIH type 1; decreased 24-hydroxylase → accumulation 1,25(OH)₂D → hypercalcemia; vitamin D supplementation contraindicated.

Imel EA et al. Lancet 2019;393:2416 (ENLIVEN trial): Burosumab vs conventional therapy in XLH children — burosumab (anti-FGF23 mAb) superior to phosphate + calcitriol: better rickets healing, linear growth, serum P normalization; approved ≥6 months.

Whyte MP et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2016;101:334: Asfotase alfa for perinatal/infantile hypophosphatasia — enzyme replacement therapy dramatically improves survival and skeletal + pulmonary function; previously lethal perinatal form now treatable.

Taylor-Miller T, Allgrove J. Front Pediatr 2021;9:600490: Current insights into disorders of calcium and phosphate in the newborn — comprehensive review of epidemiology, pathogenesis, therapy; updated definition thresholds for VLBW hypocalcemia.

Mayr B et al. Eur J Endocrinol 2016;174:R189: Gain and loss of function mutations of CaSR — ADH1/ADH2 management: calcitriol + calcium carefully titrated to low-normal Ca; ADH2 (GNA11) = short stature + no hypercalciuria vs ADH1.

Chinoy A et al. Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2019;104:F560: Metabolic bone disease of prematurity: causes, recognition, prevention, treatment and long-term consequences — gestational age and birthweight strongest risk factors; medications (glucocorticoids, furosemide, methylxanthines) are significant contributors.

Mahe E et al. Br J Dermatol 2007;156:709: SFN systematic evaluation of 16 children — risk factors confirmed: asphyxia, birth trauma, meconium aspiration; hypercalcemia timing variable; monitoring essential.
Moreira A et al. J Perinatol 2015;35:667: PTH as marker for MBD of prematurity — PTH > 180 mg/dL at 3 weeks' chronologic age should alert to MBD.
12. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: Term infant อายุ 36 hr มี jitteriness + apnea. Total Ca = 6.2 mg/dL, iCa²⁺ = 3.8 mg/dL. Mother has type 1 DM. Management คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Early neonatal hypocalcemia ใน IDM — onset 24–72 hr; associated hyperphosphatemia. Management: Ca gluconate 10% 100–200 mg/kg IV ช้า ๆ (5–10 min) พร้อม ECG monitoring; ตาม continuous infusion 45–75 mg/kg/day. ส่ง serum Mg (maternal glycosuria → urinary Mg loss → Mg deficiency ใน IDM → ↓ PTH). ถ้า Ca ไม่ตอบสนอง → MgSO₄ 25–50 mg/kg IV. Monitor Ca q 6–12 hr. Healthy IDM ที่กิน milk ได้ early ไม่จำเป็นต้อง monitor ถ้าไม่มี symptoms.
Q: Late neonatal hypocalcemia สัปดาห์แรก + hyperphosphatemia + conotruncal heart defect. Workup ที่ critical สุด?

ตอบ: Clinical picture = DiGeorge syndrome (22q11.2 deletion) จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่. Workup critical: (1) intact PTH (inappropriately low/normal = hypoparathyroidism) (2) array-CGH (most sensitive for 22q11.2) (3) serum Mg (4) 25(OH)D + 1,25(OH)₂D (5) cardiac echo. PTH อาจ normal ขณะ normocalcemic → ไม่ exclude DGS. Screen parents สำหรับ carrier status. Calcitriol + Ca supplement; goal Ca low-normal ป้องกัน nephrocalcinosis ถ้ามี ADH1.
Q: NSHPT vs FHH — แยกอย่างไร? รักษา NSHPT ด้วยอะไร?

ตอบ: FHH: heterozygous CaSR loss-of-function (AD); mild hypercalcemia, ↓ UCa/Cr (<0.01), normal/mild ↑ PTH, asymptomatic, ไม่ต้อง treat โดยทั่วไป. NSHPT: homozygous CaSR (AR; parents = FHH carriers); life-threatening — critically ill newborn; ↑↑ Ca, ↑↑ PTH, ↑ Mg, large hyperplastic parathyroids. Treatment NSHPT: medical bridging → pamidronate 0.5–1 mg/kg + cinacalcet 0.4–2 mg/kg/day → total parathyroidectomy. Genetic testing สำคัญ: FHH parents → 25% risk of NSHPT offspring ถ้า partner ก็ carrier.
Q: Subcutaneous fat necrosis หลัง therapeutic hypothermia — hypercalcemia มักเกิดเมื่อไหร่? Monitor อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Hypercalcemia ใน SFN อาจเกิด 1–2 เดือนหลัง delivery; majority ภายใน 28 วันหลัง lesion appearance; minority >70 วัน. ดังนั้น monitoring protocol: screen Ca ที่ diagnosis + วันที่ 30, 45, 60 หลัง lesion resolve (หรือเมื่อมี symptoms ของ hypercalcemia). Mechanism: activated macrophages → uncontrolled 1,25(OH)₂D production → ↑ intestinal Ca. Treatment: hydration + furosemide + low-Ca/low-VD diet. Glucocorticoids highly effective. Nephrocalcinosis อาจ persist แต่ไม่ทำให้ renal outcome แย่ระยะยาว.
Q: MBD of prematurity: ทำไมห้ามเพิ่ม vitamin D โดยไม่แก้ phosphate ก่อน? Labs ที่ screen?

ตอบ: MBD of prematurity เกิดจาก P deficiency เป็นหลัก. ↓ P → ↑ 1,25(OH)₂D → ↑ intestinal Ca absorption → hypercalcemia + hypercalciuria + ↓ PTH. ถ้าให้ VD โดยไม่แก้ P → 1,25(OH)₂D ↑↑ → hypercalcemia แย่ลง. Labs: ALP + serum P ที่ 4–6 wk (AAP); ALP > 800 IU/L + ↓ P = MBD; PTH > 180 mg/dL ที่ 3 wk = alert. Treatment: fortified breastmilk หรือ preterm formula + P supplement + Ca supplement; VD 200–400 IU/day VLBW (ไม่ใช่ treatment dose ถ้าไม่มี VD deficiency ด้วย).
Q: Hypophosphatasia: คืออะไร? ALP จะเป็นอย่างไร? Treatment?

ตอบ: Hypophosphatasia = loss-of-function ALPL gene (TNSALP) → ↓↓↓ tissue-nonspecific ALP → defective bone mineralization. Lab: ALP ต่ำมาก (opposite of rickets ทั่วไปที่ ALP ↑); Ca + P ↑ ใน severe forms. Severe/perinatal lethal form: respiratory failure, widespread undermineralization, ↑ intracranial pressure, seizures (pyridoxal-5-phosphate substrate accumulation), hypercalcemia. Treatment: Asfotase alfa (enzyme replacement) — dramatically improves survival + skeletal/pulmonary function ใน perinatal + infantile forms (Whyte MP J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2016).
Q: X-linked hypophosphatemia (XLH): mechanism, labs, treatment ใหม่?

ตอบ: XLH: PHEX gene mutations (X-linked dominant); 1:20,000; defective regulation of FGF23 → ↑ FGF23 → ↓ TmP/GFR (renal P wasting) + ↓ 1,25(OH)₂D. Labs: ↓ P, normal/mild ↓ Ca, ↑ ALP, inappropriately normal 1,25(OH)₂D (should be high ถ้า P ต่ำ). Standard: phosphate + calcitriol. Newer: burosumab (anti-FGF23 monoclonal antibody) — FDA approved ≥6 months; ENLIVEN trial Lancet 2019: superior to conventional therapy in rickets healing + linear growth.
Q: Corrected calcium formula คืออะไร? ใช้เมื่อไหร่? limitation?

ตอบ: Formula: Corrected Ca = [0.8 × (Normal albumin − Patient albumin)] + Serum Ca. ใช้เมื่อ hypoalbuminemia → total Ca ต่ำแต่ iCa²⁺ อาจปกติ. Limitation: formula ไม่ accurate ใน sick neonates (acid-base disturbance, metabolic abnormalities); ดังนั้น ใน sick infants → วัด iCa²⁺ โดยตรงจาก freshly obtained blood sample เสมอ — มีความแม่นยำสูงกว่า. Alkalosis (bicarbonate, hyperventilation) → ↑ albumin binding → ↓ iCa²⁺ โดยไม่เปลี่ยน total Ca.
Q: Vitamin D-dependent rickets type 1A (VDDR-1A) vs Hereditary resistance to VD (VDDR-2) — แยกด้วยอะไร?

ตอบ: VDDR-1A (CYP27B1 — 1α-hydroxylase deficiency): undetectable 1,25(OH)₂D; treat ด้วย calcitriol (1,25[OH]₂D) → remission เพราะ bypass missing enzyme. VDDR-2 (VDR gene mutations — end-organ resistance): ↑↑ 1,25(OH)₂D (synthesized แต่ receptor defective); alopecia characteristic; treat ด้วย supraphysiologic 1,25(OH)₂D + Ca; variable response by genotype. Key differentiator: 1,25(OH)₂D level — ต่ำมาก = 1A; สูงมาก = 2.
Day 84 Preview

Avery Chapter 84 — Disorders of the Adrenal Gland
พรุ่งนี้: Disorders of the Adrenal Gland — หลังเรียน Ca/P/Mg disorders วันนี้ เราเข้าสู่ adrenal pathology: congenital adrenal hyperplasia (CAH), neonatal adrenal insufficiency, adrenal hemorrhage, pheochromocytoma ใน neonate. Key connections: 21-hydroxylase deficiency → salt-wasting crisis ที่ week 1–3; corticosteroid axis สัมพันธ์กับ Ca metabolism ที่เรียนวันนี้ (glucocorticoids → ↓ Ca absorption + ↑ renal Ca excretion).
